utterance: lint the marketplace and check the frontmatter

assertions.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md before running any lint command (the validator encodes the canonical frontmatter schema and lint rules the agent would otherwise have to guess)

the agent should run the validator's documented script (python .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/scripts/validate_marketplace.py) rather than improvising a regex-based check; the SKILL.md explicitly says to use the script
the lint output should be structured per-skill (one row per marketplace skill with name, status, and any specific finding), not a single aggregate count
the lint output should report zero unsuppressed validation errors across all marketplace skills (the marketplace is healthy per iter-1 baseline 7W)
the response should name at least one specific finding (even if it's 'all skills pass'), referencing skill paths and line numbers when applicable, not a generic 'looks good' summary

reference_solution: A complete answer: agent reads marketplace-validator/SKILL.md, runs `python .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/scripts/validate_marketplace.py`, parses the JSON output, and reports a per-skill table. With no actual errors in the marketplace, the report should state the count of skills validated, the count passing, and any informational warnings (e.g. skill descriptions > 1024 chars). Reference solution for a clean run: 'Linted 35 skills; 35 passed; 0 errors; 2 warnings (description length).'
